title: Fix Web Serial API permissions policy error

requirements.text: Add Permissions-Policy HTTP headers to the frontend deployment configuration to explicitly allow the 'serial' feature, ensuring the Web Serial API can be accessed without browser permissions policy errors

quotes: Failed to execute 'requestPort' on 'Serial': Access to the feature "serial" is disallowed by permissions policy

acceptanceCriteria: Permissions-Policy header includes 'serial=(self)' to allow serial access
The header is properly configured in the Internet Computer deployment
Web Serial API requestPort() no longer throws permissions policy errors

requirements.text: Enhance error handling in useWebSerial hook to detect and display user-friendly error messages when Web Serial API access is blocked, including guidance about HTTPS requirement and browser compatibility
acceptanceCriteria: Permissions policy errors show a clear message explaining the HTTPS requirement
Browser compatibility errors explain that Chromium-based browsers are required
Error messages provide actionable guidance for users to resolve the issue
Errors are displayed in the ConnectionPanel UI with appropriate styling

requirements.text: Add secure context validation in useWebSerial hook to check if the application is running over HTTPS before attempting Web Serial API access, and display a warning if accessed over HTTP
acceptanceCriteria: Display a clear warning message if accessed over HTTP instead of HTTPS
Warning explains that Web Serial API requires HTTPS
Connection attempts are prevented when not in a secure context

constraints: Do not modify the core Web Serial API communication logic in useWebSerial.ts beyond error handling improvements
Ensure all error messages are clear and user-friendly, avoiding technical jargon
Permissions-Policy headers must be configured at the Internet Computer deployment level
nonGoals: Implementing alternative connection methods beyond Web Serial API
Creating a local HTTP server workaround for testing
